想.origin: On top 相 gives the sound, and underneath sits 心 (heart) — when your heart thinks, you 想 (want or wish).

sentence.en: I want to eat an apple.

些.origin: Picture 此 (this) sitting above 二 (two) — pointing at just a few of something means 些 (some).

sentence.en: I want to buy some fruit.

先.origin: A foot stepping out in front of a person 儿 — whoever steps ahead goes 先 (first).

怎.origin: The heart 心 sits at the bottom, asking with worry — when your heart wonders, you ask 怎 (how).

倒.origin: A person 亻 next to 到 — when a person tips all the way over, they 倒 (fall down).

sentence.en: The kitten fell down on the ground.

筷.origin: Bamboo ⺮ on top with 快 below — bamboo sticks you eat with quickly are 筷 (chopsticks).

练.origin: Thread 纟 on the left — like working a thread again and again, you 练 (practice) until you're good.

像.origin: A person 亻 standing beside an elephant 象 — copying just how it looks means 像 (to resemble).

幅.origin: Cloth 巾 on the left with 畐 giving the sound — a hanging strip of cloth is one 幅 (piece) of art.

sentence.en: There is a painting on the wall.

弯.origin: A bow 弓 sits at the bottom — a bow is never straight, so 弯 means bent or curved.

每.origin: Think of a mother 母 caring the same way for all her children — 每 means each and every one.

侯.origin: A person 亻 standing tall like a target 矦 — an honored noble person was called a 侯 (lord).

sentence.en: In old times there were many lords.

伸.origin: A person 亻 beside 申 — reaching out long and straight, the person can 伸 (stretch).

腿.origin: Flesh ⺼ on the left with 退 (to step back) — the fleshy part you walk with is your 腿 (leg).

让.origin: Speech 讠 on the left — using kind words to step aside means 让 (to let or yield).

进.origin: The walking radical 辶 wraps around 井 — when your feet move forward, you 进 (enter or advance).

猜.origin: The dog radical 犭 on the left with 青 — sniffing around to figure something out is to 猜 (guess).

sentence.en: Can you guess how old I am?

粉.origin: Grain 米 on the left with 分 (to divide) — grain ground into tiny bits becomes 粉 (powder).

sentence.en: There is white powder on the table.

采.origin: A hand 爫 reaching down onto a tree 木 — picking fruit off the branches is to 采 (gather).

蜜.origin: The insect 虫 at the bottom — the sweet treat made by little bees is 蜜 (honey).

sentence.en: The little bear loves eating honey.

劳.origin: Strength 力 at the bottom lifting a load — using all your might to work hard is 劳 (labor).

落.origin: Grass 艹 on top with 洛 giving the sound — like a leaf coming down, 落 means to fall.

腰.origin: Flesh ⺼ on the left with 要 for the sound — the bendy middle part of your body is your 腰 (waist).

挂.origin: The hand radical 扌 on the left — your hand reaches up to 挂 (hang) something on the wall.

sentence.en: A hat is hanging on the wall.

梢.origin: Tree 木 on the left with 肖 for the sound — the very end of a tree's branch is the 梢 (treetop).

sentence.en: A bird rests on the treetop.

把.origin: The hand radical 扌 on the left — your hand reaches out to 把 (grasp and hold) something.

刀.origin: The shape itself is a picture of a blade with a handle — 刀 simply means knife.

谁.origin: Speech 讠 on the left with a little bird 隹 — when you speak to ask a name, you say 谁 (who).

吗.origin: A mouth 口 on the left with horse 马 for the sound — the mouth's little question word is 吗.

变.origin: A hand 又 at the bottom busily working — when something turns into something new, it 变 (changes).

汽.origin: Water 氵 on the left with air 气 — when water turns into floating air, it becomes 汽 (steam, gas).

飘.origin: Wind 风 on the right pushing 票 along — caught in the breeze, things 飘 (float and flutter).

sentence.en: The red flag flutters in the wind.

珠.origin: The jade radical 王 on the left with 朱 — a small round shiny gem is a 珠 (pearl or bead).

掉.origin: The hand radical 扌 on the left — letting go, the thing slips and 掉 (drops) to the floor.

湖.origin: Water 氵 on the left with 胡 for the sound — a big quiet pool of water is a 湖 (lake).

sentence.en: There are little fish in the lake.

问.origin: A mouth 口 peeking out from a door 门 — calling through the door to find out is to 问 (ask).

sentence.en: I want to ask the teacher.

渴.origin: Water 氵 on the left with 曷 for the sound — when you really need water, you feel 渴 (thirsty).

sentence.en: I'm thirsty and want some water.

死.origin: A person kneeling 匕 beside bare bones 歹 — the sad picture of an ending means 死 (dead).

啦.origin: A mouth 口 on the left with 拉 for the sound — the cheerful word your mouth adds at the end is 啦.

刚.origin: The knife radical 刂 on the right — strong and sharp like a blade, 刚 means firm or just now.
